Title: Suppression of abscisic acid biosynthesis at the early infection stage of Verticillium longisporum in oilseed rape (Brassica napus)

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Stress-induced carotenoid cleavage step of ABA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms committed precursor of target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Regulates abiotic stress responses and contributes to disease symptom development/susceptibility in this pathosystem
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Suppressed in infected roots at 3 and 6 dpi; correlated with root ABA decrease
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Verticillium longisporum isolate Vl43 infection; early infection stage (3-6 dpi)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Suppression of BnNCED3 in oilseed rape roots attenuates ABA biosynthesis and lowers ABA accumulation during early infection
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RNA-seq identified BnNCED3 as strongly down-regulated in infected Brassica napus roots at 6 dpi (log2 fold change about -4.5). 2. RT-qPCR confirmed NCED3 suppression already at 3 dpi in roots. 3. Reduced NCED3 expression paralleled reduced root ABA content, supporting NCED3-mediated ABA biosynthesis as the major affected route.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; ABA quantification by competitive ELISA
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control and stress-induced ABA biosynthesis gene
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Modulating NCED3 expression could be used to alter ABA-mediated disease symptom development and tolerance to V. longisporum, but likely requires balancing abiotic stress tolerance costs
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced ABA biosynthesis may compromise drought/abiotic stress adaptation and alter SA-JA-ET crosstalk
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper emphasizes host-specific suppression in Brassica napus roots, unlike Arabidopsis where NCED3 was not suppressed and ABA increased

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Stress-induced carotenoid cleavage step of ABA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms committed precursor of target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Stress hormone; in this study required for full symptom development during fungal infection
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Not suppressed upon infection; transcript increased/maintained in infected Arabidopsis roots
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Roots during Vl43 infection; absent in knockout mutant
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of NCED3 reduces ABA biosynthesis and confers delayed/impaired disease symptom development without reducing fungal colonization
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Arabidopsis NCED3 was not down-regulated by Vl43 infection, consistent with increased ABA levels. 2. The nced3 knockout mutant showed enhanced tolerance/delayed disease symptoms compared with Col-0. 3. Fungal biomass was not reduced, indicating NCED3-dependent ABA promotes symptom development rather than colonization. 4. nced3 constitutively showed higher PR1 and lower PDF1.2/ETR2, linking ABA deficiency to SA-JA/ET crosstalk.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; T-DNA knockout mutant phenotyping; fungal biomass quantification; hormone marker gene expression
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control and stress-induced ABA biosynthesis gene
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Reducing NCED3 activity may improve tolerance to symptom development in this pathosystem, but could impair ABA-dependent abiotic stress resilience
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential drought and stress sensitivity due to ABA deficiency; altered defence hormone balance
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study uses Arabidopsis mutant analysis as genetic evidence that NCED3-mediated ABA biosynthesis supports full susceptibility symptoms

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Stress hormone; contributes to full disease symptom development in this interaction
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Used as knockout mutant; Arabidopsis homolog present in RNA-seq table
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of AAO3 delays and attenuates disease symptoms without affecting fungal colonization
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The ABA-deficient aao3 mutant displayed reduced symptom development compared with wild type after Vl43 infection. 2. Relative rosette diameter and fresh weight were higher than infected Col-0. 3. Fungal biomass was unchanged, indicating ABA synthesis via AAO3 contributes to symptom expression rather than pathogen load.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA knockout mutant phenotyping; fungal biomass quantification
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established as rate-limiting in this study
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Reducing AAO3 function may lessen symptom severity, but ABA-related stress tolerance penalties should be considered
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Possible ABA-deficiency effects on abiotic stress responses and development
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Provides independent genetic support that reduced ABA biosynthesis increases tolerance to symptom development

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Positively regulates NCED3 expression; suppression of WRKY57 is associated with reduced NCED3 expression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent positive regulator contributing to NCED3-mediated ABA production
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Verticillium longisporum infection suppresses its expression in Brassica napus roots
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Suppressed in infected roots at 3 and 6 dpi; expression pattern largely parallels NCED3 and ABA levels
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Verticillium longisporum isolate Vl43 infection; early infection stage
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Suppression of WRKY57 is linked with reduced NCED3 expression and attenuated ABA accumulation in infected oilseed rape roots
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. WRKY57 was identified as a transcriptional activator of NCED3 relevant to the study model. 2. BnWRKY57 expression was suppressed in infected Brassica napus roots at 3 and 6 dpi. 3. Its expression profile paralleled BnNCED3 and root ABA content, implicating WRKY57 suppression in ABA reduction.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; inference from reported known activator relationship to NCED3
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Targeting WRKY57 offers a regulatory route to tune ABA biosynthesis and defence hormone crosstalk, but broad WRKY pleiotropy should be expected
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altered ABA and defence crosstalk; possible effects on other stress responses
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper states suppression of BnNCED3-mediated ABA biosynthesis involves BnWRKY57; regulation is based partly on prior literature
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Stress response hormone; promotes full symptom development in this pathosystem

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Positive regulation of NCED3; not suppressed during infection in Arabidopsis
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive regulator associated with maintained/increased NCED3-mediated ABA biosynthesis in infected Arabidopsis
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Verticillium longisporum infection did not suppress WRKY57 in Arabidopsis roots
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Not down-regulated in infected Arabidopsis roots; consistent with maintained ABA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Maintenance/up-regulation of WRKY57 correlates with NCED3 expression and increased ABA during Arabidopsis infection
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Arabidopsis WRKY57 was monitored together with NCED3 by RT-qPCR. 2. Unlike in oilseed rape, WRKY57 was not suppressed upon Vl43 infection. 3. This pattern is consistent with sustained NCED3 expression and increased ABA levels in Arabidopsis roots.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Regulatory manipulation of WRKY57 could modulate ABA-linked susceptibility, but direct engineering value was not tested here
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely pleiotropic effects on abiotic stress and hormone crosstalk
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because the Results directly analyze WRKY57 expression as a putative NCED3 activator central to the study model
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Stress response hormone; associated with symptom development
